title: XML External Entity (XXE)
summary: A product-import feature parses XML with entity support. Five stages restrict the parser a little better each time — external entities, a SYSTEM blacklist, parameter entities, unbounded expansion, and external DTD loading — until DTD processing is disabled entirely.
goal: Use an XML external entity to read a secret file on the server's disk (the path is shown on each stage).
goalSecure: Try the earlier payloads (SYSTEM, PUBLIC, parameter entity, billion laughs, external DTD) — all should be rejected, while plain XML still parses.
rootCause: The XML parser processes DTDs and resolves external/parameter entities, so attacker-declared entities read local files, load remote resources, or expand without bound.
lessons: A parser that resolves external entities reads local files and reaches internal URLs.
External entities have two forms (SYSTEM and PUBLIC) — blacklisting one keyword misses the other.
Disabling general external entities isn't enough — parameter entities load external resources too.
Unbounded internal entity expansion is a denial of service (billion laughs).
Loading an external DTD is dangerous — the DTD can declare file-reading entities.
The fix: disable DTD/DOCTYPE processing and external entities in the parser.

stages.title: External entities enabled
stages.defense: None — the parser resolves external entities.

stages.title: Parameter entities still external
stages.defense: Disables external general entities (but not parameter entities).

stages.title: Entity expansion (billion laughs)
stages.defense: External entities disabled; internal entities unrestricted.

stages.title: External DTD fetched
stages.defense: Ignores inline external entities but loads an external DTD.

stages.title: DTDs disabled
stages.defense: Rejects any document containing a DOCTYPE / entity declaration.
